L1_ledger.primitive: SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE on a dedicated connection

L5_ledger_lock_VESTIGIAL_BUT_LIVE.excludes: *** NOTHING *** — mutually invisible to L1's BEGIN IMMEDIATE
L5_ledger_lock_VESTIGIAL_BUT_LIVE.verdict: F-B (Cycle 2): a live writer's commit() SUCCEEDS and its data is SILENTLY DISCARDED

cardinal_rule_honoured: No network call and no heavy subprocess ever runs inside L1. Verified at every site.
L1_L2_exclusion: NONE (COUP-01). Handled for the one case that matters: publish re-resolves the integration id at publish time and FINALIZE merges account_id with an explicit 'in-flight wins' policy.

network_call_sites.http_library: requests — the SOLE HTTP library in src/fanops

sites.why: the object key is fanops/{sha256(file)[:32]}{suffix} (postiz.py:171) — CONTENT-ADDRESSED, so a re-upload overwrites the same object
NOT content-addressed — a retry creates a SECOND media object. An orphaned-media leak, NOT a double-publish.

sites.role: POSTIZ STATUS (a +/-35d date window — Postiz has NO per-post status endpoint)

sites.unknown_id_behaviour: *** RAISES RuntimeError on any non-2xx, INCLUDING a 404 *** — this asymmetry with Postiz is the root of C3-F2

sites.call: Meta Graph (injectable `get` so tests never touch the network)

clip.py.what: ffmpeg render / crop / concat / subtitle burn

llm.py.what: claude -p — THE LLM IS A SUBPROCESS, NOT AN HTTP API. It rides the operator's existing `claude login`; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is NOT required, and `claude --bare -p` provably FAILS because it never reads the keychain (llm.py:12-23).

post/compress.py.lock: *** NONE — runs in the LOCK-FREE NETWORK phase of _publish_one ***

postiz_lifecycle.py.what: docker — SHELLED FROM publish_due (run.py:447-449) when `due` is non-empty

publish_side_effect_ordering.op: BEGIN IMMEDIATE; guard state is queued AND due; post.state = submitting

publish_side_effect_ordering.note: *** F11 — the claim is persisted BEFORE any network I/O ***

publish_side_effect_ordering.defect: C3-F4 mkdtemp leak + C3-F5 Render.path rewritten into it

publish_side_effect_ordering.idempotent: R2 yes (content-addressed key); Postiz multipart NO

publish_side_effect_ordering.hazard: the throttle dict is per-PROCESS. N concurrent publisher processes publish N x the rate limit.

publish_side_effect_ordering.kind: *** THE EXTERNAL EFFECT. NOT IDEMPOTENT. NO KEY. ***

publish_side_effect_ordering.op: BEGIN IMMEDIATE; merge ONLY _NET_POST_FIELDS into a FRESHLY LOADED ledger; clip/render media cache (first-writer-wins); renders[rid].path <- the mkdtemp path

publish_side_effect_ordering.note: OUTSIDE the txn, fail-open — a full disk must never roll back a committed publish

crash_critical_window: T2 -> T8. A crash anywhere in it leaves `submitting`, which publish_due never re-drives. THAT IS CORRECT. The defect is that reconcile — the only READER of `submitting` — cannot always terminate it (C3-F1 / C3-F2).

filesystem_effects.mitigation: the FLOCK (the authority) is kernel-released on death, so the LEASE self-heals; only the advisory body is lost

filesystem_effects.note: the .mp4 SUFFIX IS REQUIRED — it picks the muxer (COUP-07 / MOL-78)
filesystem_effects.cleanup: cascade unlink, DEFERRED until AFTER commit (ledger.py:487-488) — a rolled-back txn never deletes a file it did not drop

filesystem_effects.primitive: O_CREAT|O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC, 0o600 — created 0600 ATOMICALLY, no world-readable window
WAL + synchronous=FULL + full-document replace in one BEGIN IMMEDIATE; chmod 0o600 after every write

filesystem_effects.cleanup: *** NEVER — C3-F4. The ONLY mkdtemp in src/fanops, with NO rmtree anywhere in the tree. And the ledger's Render.path is rewritten INTO it (C3-F5). ***
purge_source_artifacts only, on an explicit T0 reset

Clip.media_url / Render.media_url (the F.verdict: C3-F11 — if the hosted URL expires, nothing invalidates the cache; a dead CDN URL is reused forever. FALSIFIES brief section 9 claim #9.

media_cache_validity_across_backends.key: _media_cache_hit identifies a Postiz URL BY IT CONTAINING A LITERAL '|' (media.py:58)

sites.note: SECRETS go to the OS KEYRING ONLY, never plaintext .env. The ONLY writer of FANOPS_LIVE (INV-18, upheld).

propagation.daemon: re-reads .env (override=True) AND rebuilds Config EVERY TICK (cli.py:1303-1304)
propagation.studio: load_dotenv ONCE at process entry (cli.py:795), then blocks in app.run (cli.py:1285) — a .env change made by the CLI or the daemon NEVER reaches a running Studio (COUP-02b)
propagation.config: properties are os.getenv PER ACCESS (74 sites, uncached), so a go_live write is visible to the WRITING process immediately
